chunk_map.heading: # Reading the return (title slide, no lede)
What would have caught this earlier?
Three failure modes you'll use to read
Ronacher's ten-hour port surfaced all three

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 3 "Why these three, every run" line 18 ("The three are consequences, not bad luck") and chunk 4 "Ronacher's ten-hour port surfaced all three" line 25 ("all three failure modes") both reuse the trio (goal drift / context rot / plausible-but-wrong) named in chunk 2, which precedes both under a sequential deck read. Not a violation — reuse of a term introduced on an earlier slide of the same file is explicitly the NOT-a-violation case in compendium rule 1.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
Live-demo beats survive a text read.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 3's and chunk 4's headers read true under the sequential deck read (the trio was named on chunk 2), consistent with the rule-1 disposition above.

Checked all five sub-items across all chunks. chunk 1 line 6 ("Not *did the run succeed*. Not *should I have spec'd it tighter*. A diagnostic:...") is two "Not X" clauses followed by a reframing sentence, not the banned "Not X. Not Y. Z." declarative-collapse construction (the third clause is a redirect to method, not a Z-declarative naming the thing) — read against the compendium's own example ("Not X. Not Y. Z.") this is a looser rhetorical question pattern, kept as accept-with-note rather than a flag. No anaphora triples, no self-grading superlatives, no number+retraction, no abstraction-noun stacks, no empty-leg verb triplets found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: "the map" / "the Work phase" (chunk 3) are arc-earned AE101 spine vocabulary (the training's own named framework, carried across modules — the-far-half.md lecture is titled "The far half of the map"), not maintainer-only dialect. Carve-out applies.
